behavior_in_task_description.reason: instruction.md names js/atoms/src/Block.svelte, .hide-container:not(.fullscreen), .block, --block-border-width, and template attributes class:hide-container, class:fullscreen, style:flex-grow, <slot, matching all test assertions.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction explicitly dictates the fix: 'must set its own border directly to zero, without using --block-border-width', 'must NOT set --block-border-width: 0', '.block must set border-width: var(--block-border-width)', and 'template must NOT apply border-width via inline style' — these are the exact diff lines.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests check the structural properties (no inheritable var, direct border-width: 0, .block has border-width var, no inline style:border-width) which any correct fix per the spec would share.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: While CSS-text checks exist, repo_tests run pnpm format:check and pnpm test:run js/atoms/Block.test.ts which execute the unit test suite.

test_not_tautological.reason: f2p tests require both removing --block-border-width: 0, adding direct border-width: 0, and removing the inline style attribute — a stub cannot satisfy these.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All 7 verification points in instruction.md map to assertions in test_outputs.py.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are clearly grouped with comment headers '=== fail_to_pass ===', '=== pass_to_pass ===', '=== repo_tests ===' and named descriptively.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task is a CSS/Svelte fix, not structured data output.

no_typos.reason: Paths js/atoms/src/Block.svelte and CSS class names are consistent across instruction, tests, and solution.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction names the exact file js/atoms/src/Block.svelte and dictates the precise CSS lines to add/remove (e.g., 'must NOT set --block-border-width: 0', '.block must set border-width: var(--block-border-width)'), eliminating localization.
